info.title: x402 Singularity Layer API
info.description: OpenAPI-backed reference for marketplace discovery, payment routes, webhooks, wallet-first auth, agent endpoints, and ERC-8004 flows.

get.description: Lists public endpoints, products, and agentic listings with optional filters.

get.summary: Get public credit packs for an endpoint
Access a paid endpoint or return an x402 challenge
Access a paid product or return an x402 challenge
Resolve a signed payment receipt token
List ratings for an endpoint or product

api_schema.description: Optional API schema defining routes and parameters. Displayed to consumers on the payment page and in marketplace listings.

get.summary: Get a single public marketplace agent

post.description: Returns x402 and MPP 402 payment challenges when no payment credential is supplied, or provisions a Vultr or DigitalOcean instance after x402 or MPP payment settlement.

prepaid_hours.description: Prepaid runtime in hours (minimum 24). Provision uses prepaid_hours; the extend endpoint uses extend_hours.

use_credits.description: Pay from prepaid USD credits instead of an x402 wallet payment. Supported for bare and grid machines. NOT supported for ai_machine (private AI Machines require an x402 wallet payment).

provider.description: Optional provider hint. Plan IDs with do: prefix resolve to DigitalOcean.

network.description: x402 payment network. Robinhood Chain settles in USDG; Base and MegaETH settle in USDC; Solana settles in USDC. MPP clients use Authorization: Payment and do not need this field.

user_data.description: Optional cloud-init user data run at first boot.

ai_machine.description: Private AI Machine (Standard tier). When present, the machine self-installs the pinned LLM behind your own OpenAI-compatible endpoint (URL + API key), running llama-server (llama.cpp) on port 8080 exposing /v1 (/v1/chat/completions and /v1/models). Supports x402, MPP, or prepaid credits. Mutually exclusive with deploy_node.

model_id.description: The LLM to serve on the instance.

deploy_node.description: Join the grid. When present, the machine self-installs as a network node owned by the paying wallet, earning USDC + SGL (requires 50,000 SGL staked). Requires an x402 Solana payment. Mutually exclusive with ai_machine.

model_id.description: The LLM the node will serve on the grid.

tee_type.description: Optional TEE type for the node deploy.

402.description: Payment required. x402 clients use the JSON body. MPP clients use the WWW-Authenticate: Payment header.

extend_hours.description: Additional prepaid runtime in hours (minimum 24). This endpoint uses extend_hours; provision uses prepaid_hours.

network.description: x402 payment network. MPP clients use Authorization: Payment and do not need this field.

402.description: Payment required. x402 clients use the JSON body. MPP clients use the WWW-Authenticate: Payment header.

post.description: Resizes an active instance on its current provider. The route preserves the remaining prepaid dollar credit and recalculates expiry for the target hourly rate.

plan.description: Target plan id from GET /compute/plans. DigitalOcean plans use the do: prefix.

confirm_disk_resize.description: Required when the target resize grows disk capacity, especially for DigitalOcean where disk growth is irreversible.

400.description: Unsupported resize target or missing confirmation

409.description: Instance is not active or already has a resize in progress

get.description: Returns all publicly visible fundraiser campaigns with pagination and an agent metadata block for programmatic discovery.

200.description: Campaign list with pagination and agent metadata

post.description: Creates a new fundraiser campaign. Requires Supabase authentication.

get.description: Returns a single campaign with contributions and agent metadata including payment instructions for programmatic contribution.

200.description: Campaign detail with contributions and agent payment instructions

patch.description: Edit a campaign you own. Only `title`, `description`, `x_handle`, `images`, and `links` can be modified. Blocked once the campaign is `funded`, `launched`, or `expired`.

get.description: Returns up to 200 contributions for a campaign, ordered by amount (highest first). No authentication required.

post.description: Record a contribution after a successful x402 payment. The server verifies a signed receipt JWT (from the `X-X402-Receipt-Token` response header) bound to the specific fundraiser endpoint before accepting the contribution. No separate authentication required — the receipt token is the proof of payment.

receipt_token.description: Signed x402 receipt token from the `X-X402-Receipt-Token` response header after payment

display_name.description: Public name shown in the supporters list

fee_share_eligible.description: Whether this contributor is within the first 100 unique wallets

400.description: Invalid receipt, campaign closed, or minimum not met ($2.50)

get.description: Returns the 20 most recent contributions across all campaigns. Used for the live ticker display.
Returns up to 100 comments on a campaign, ordered newest first. No authentication required.

post.description: Post a comment on a campaign. Requires Supabase authentication.
Upload a campaign image. Requires authentication. Max 1 MB. Allowed types: JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF. Validated by magic bytes, not just MIME type.

get.description: Returns a dynamically generated 1200×630 PNG Open Graph image showing campaign status, title, raised amount, progress bar, and backer count. Used for social media previews.
Returns your enterprise partner configuration including pricing overrides and revenue split percentages.
Returns all endpoints deployed through your enterprise program with pagination.
Returns an aggregated revenue summary for your enterprise program including three-way split totals.
Returns the full revenue ledger showing every payment and the three-way split between enterprise partner, Singularity, and infrastructure.
Self-describing manifest of the whole agentic staking API and the prepare→sign→submit flow. An agent can fetch this to discover every endpoint.
Network-wide stats: total staked, staker count, TVL, and reward-pool totals.
Stake positions for a wallet across all roles: amount, status, cooldowns, and claimable rewards. Public on-chain data.

post.description: Begins the cooldown. After it elapses, call /api/agent/claim-unstake to withdraw.
Only succeeds after the cooldown has elapsed (see positions.claimableUnlock).
Claims both USDC and $SGL rewards owed. Provide a role for a single position, or omit role to claim every position with rewards (returns multiple transactions).
Submits a base64 signed Solana transaction (from a prepare endpoint, signed with your keypair), confirms it, and returns the signature.

get.description: List the models currently served by active attested nodes. OpenAI-compatible shape. Each model includes `context_window` — the maximum prompt + output tokens the grid will accept for it right now (the largest live node's context). A request whose `prompt + max_tokens` exceeds this is rejected with a `400 context_length_exceeded` before any charge, and the router only dispatches a request to a node whose context actually fits it.

parameters.name: X-API-Key
parameters.description: Compute/Grid API key (billed to prepaid credits). Omit to pay per request via x402 `X-Payment`.

context_window.description: Maximum prompt + output tokens accepted for this model right now (the largest live node's context window). Size requests against this; exceeding it returns `400 context_length_exceeded` before any charge.

post.description: OpenAI-compatible chat completion. Set `"stream": true` for token streaming (SSE; each chunk is end-to-end encrypted). Works with any OpenAI SDK via `base_url=https://grid.x402compute.cc/v1`. Authenticate with `X-API-Key` (prepaid credits) or pay per request with the x402 `X-Payment` header after a `402` challenge.

parameters.name: X-API-Key
parameters.description: Compute/Grid API key (billed to prepaid credits). Omit to pay per request via x402 `X-Payment`.

402.description: Payment required — resend with `X-Payment`, or top up credits in Settings → Credits

get.description: Live public network stats — node counts by hardware, lifetime jobs served/submitted, total $SGL staked, per-model availability. No auth, open CORS.
Prepaid USDC credit balance for the authenticated wallet. Requires signature auth (`X-Auth-*`) or an API key (`X-API-Key`).

parameters.name: X-API-Key
parameters.description: Compute/Grid API key (billed to prepaid credits). Omit to pay per request via x402 `X-Payment`.

post.description: Top up prepaid credits via x402 payment. Returns `402 Payment Required` if no `X-Payment` header is present; resend with the payment payload.

parameters.name: X-API-Key
parameters.description: Compute/Grid API key (billed to prepaid credits). Omit to pay per request via x402 `X-Payment`.

get.description: Public, no auth. Returns the deployable agents, AI modes, managed tiers with their model lists, per-model metadata (live price/context/vision + best-for), memory backends, pricing margins, and runtime feature flags (trial, uncensored, memory). Read this before `POST /pods` to pick a valid `agent_id`, `ai_mode`, `tier`, and `model`.

models.description: Curated model slugs this tier may switch among at runtime.

model_meta.description: Map of model slug -> metadata (id, name, price_in, price_out, context, vision, best_for, uncensored). Prices are USD per 1M tokens.

trial_enabled.description: Whether the free 24h trial is currently offered.

pod_byok_margin_pct.description: BYOK service premium as a fraction of the CPU cost (e.g. 0.10 = 10%).

pod_managed_margin_pct.description: Managed service fee as a fraction of the machine cost (e.g. 0.20 = 20%).

get.description: Returns every Agent Pod owned by the authenticated wallet. Authenticate with the same compute auth as `/compute/*`: an API key (`X-API-Key: x402c_...`), a session bearer (`Authorization: Bearer sgl_sess.<token>`), or a wallet signature (`X-Auth-Address`, `X-Auth-Chain`, `X-Auth-Signature`, `X-Auth-Nonce`, `X-Auth-Timestamp`).

parameters.name: X-API-Key
parameters.description: Compute API key. Alternatively use a session bearer or wallet-signature headers.

post.description: Deploys a hosted agent. The paid deploy rides the audited `/compute/provision` money path, so payment works exactly like provisioning a machine: pay from prepaid credits (`use_credits: true`) or complete an x402/MPP payment (a `402` challenge is returned when no payment credential is supplied). All `/compute/provision` fields (`plan`, `region`, `prepaid_hours`, `network`, `ssh_public_key`, ...) are accepted alongside the pod-specific fields below; `os_id` is ignored (pods always run Ubuntu 24.04). Authenticate with the same compute auth as `/compute/*` (API key, session bearer, or wallet signature).

parameters.name: X-API-Key
parameters.description: Compute API key. Alternatively use a session bearer or wallet-signature headers.

ai_mode.description: `managed` = we run the LLM (metered, tier-driven). `byok` = the agent uses your own OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

tier.description: Required when `ai_mode` is `managed`. A tier id from `GET /pods/catalog` (drives the model + machine floor).

model.description: Optional model slug. Managed: honored only if it is in the chosen tier's list, else the tier default. BYOK: any slug.

plan.description: Compute plan id from `GET /compute/plans`. DigitalOcean plans use the `do:` prefix.

plan_ram_mb.description: Optional. Validated against the agent's (and tier's) RAM floor.

use_credits.description: Pay from prepaid USD credits instead of an x402 wallet payment.

network.description: x402 payment network. MPP clients use `Authorization: Payment` and omit this.

llm_api_key.description: Required when `ai_mode` is `byok`. Your provider API key (stored encrypted).

llm_base_url.description: BYOK only. Your OpenAI-compatible base URL. Defaults to `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`.

channels.description: Optional channel tokens, keyed by channel id (e.g. `{ "telegram": "<bot-token>" }`). Must be supported by the agent.

ssh_public_key.description: Optional. Rarely needed — pods are headless.

200.description: Pod deployed. Returns the provision result (including `order`) merged with a `pod` object. For managed pods, `pod.managed_ai_key` is shown ONCE.

id.description: Pod id (equals the compute order id).

managed_ai_key.description: Managed pods only. The pod's key for our managed LLM proxy. Shown once.

400.description: Invalid body (unknown agent, bad `ai_mode`, missing `tier`, machine too small, ...)

402.description: Payment required. x402 clients use the JSON body; MPP clients use the `WWW-Authenticate: Payment` header.

409.description: Agent is coming soon and can't be deployed yet.

503.description: Feature not configured (e.g. managed AI or pod secrets disabled).

get.description: Returns a single pod you own, including its latest heartbeat and (when enabled) masked delegated-credential state. Authenticate with the same compute auth as `/compute/*` (API key, session bearer, or wallet signature).

credentials.description: Masked delegated-credential state (never raw keys). Null unless delegated credentials are enabled.

404.description: Pod not found (or not owned by you).

patch.description: Owner-tunable pod knobs. Send any subset of the fields below. `heartbeat_interval_sec` applies live (no restart); `model` triggers an agent restart (~40s); `auto_renew_enabled` controls lifecycle billing. Authenticate with the same compute auth as `/compute/*`.

heartbeat_interval_sec.description: Pod worker poll interval in seconds.

auto_renew_enabled.description: Renew this pod from your credits at expiry. Rejected for free-trial pods.

model.description: Model slug like `provider/model` (e.g. `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Applies to chat, channels, and the OpenAI adapter.

400.description: Invalid field, or nothing to update.

get.description: Recent managed-AI usage for the pod plus aggregates, including the funding-source split (included AI allowance vs Singularity credits). Authenticate with the same compute auth as `/compute/*`.

allowance_usd.description: Portion paid from the pod's included AI allowance.

get.description: Lists the pod's OpenAI-adapter integration keys (`sk-sglpod-int-*`) in masked form. Requires the OpenAI adapter to be enabled. Authenticate with the same compute auth as `/compute/*`.

404.description: Adapter not enabled, or pod not found.

post.summary: Create an Agent Pod integration key
post.description: Creates a new integration key for the pod's OpenAI adapter. The raw key (`sk-sglpod-int-...`) is returned ONCE and never again. Use it as a bearer token against `/pods/{id}/v1/*`. Requires the OpenAI adapter to be enabled. Authenticate with the same compute auth as `/compute/*`.

key.description: The raw key. Shown once — store it now.

404.description: Adapter not enabled, or pod not found.

delete.summary: Revoke an Agent Pod integration key
delete.description: Revokes an integration key. Idempotent. Requires the OpenAI adapter to be enabled. Authenticate with the same compute auth as `/compute/*`.

404.description: Adapter not enabled, or pod not found.

get.description: OpenAI-compatible model list for the pod. Always returns the single model `agent-pod`. Authenticate with a pod integration key as a bearer token: `Authorization: Bearer sk-sglpod-int-<key>` (create one with `POST /pods/{id}/api-keys`).

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Pod integration key as a bearer token.

404.description: Adapter not enabled, or pod unavailable.

post.description: Runs ONE agent turn on your pod and returns an OpenAI `chat.completion`. Non-streaming only: `stream:true` is rejected with `400 stream_unsupported`. `usage` is always `null` (token counts are not faked in v1). Point any OpenAI SDK at `https://compute.x402layer.cc/pods/{id}/v1` with `model="agent-pod"`. Authenticate with a pod integration key as a bearer token: `Authorization: Bearer sk-sglpod-int-<key>`.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Pod integration key as a bearer token.

stream.description: Must be false or omitted. `true` returns `400 stream_unsupported`.

404.description: Adapter not enabled, or pod unavailable (`pod_unavailable`).

429.description: Daily request cap reached for this key (`daily_cap`).

502.description: Agent could not complete the request.

get.description: Models currently served with node count, average reputation, and per-token pricing. No auth.
Unclaimed on-chain Merkle reward epochs for an operator wallet (their 80% share). Read-only; safe to expose — claiming still requires the wallet to sign. Base58, never lowercased.
Registered node records for an operator wallet. No auth (public node metadata, no secrets).

anchored.description: Whether the operator verified (anchored) this node's key on-chain.

get.description: Time-series of network staking metrics. No auth.

PodView.description: Public view of an Agent Pod (never leaks secrets).

id.description: Pod id (equals the compute order id).

PodApiKey.description: Masked Agent Pod integration key (never the raw key or hash).

api_schema.description: Optional API schema describing available routes, parameters, and request bodies
API schema describing available routes and parameters for this endpoint
When present, describes the API routes, parameters, and request bodies the endpoint accepts. Agents can use this to self-discover how to call the API after payment.

CampaignListAgentMeta.description: Agent metadata for programmatic campaign discovery

CampaignDetailAgentMeta.description: Agent metadata with payment instructions for programmatic contribution

payment_endpoint.description: x402-compatible payment URL. Send USDC here.

remaining.description: USDC still needed to reach the target

fee_share_eligible.description: Whether new contributors can still earn fee-sharing

fee_share_slots_remaining.description: How many of the 100 fee-share slots are still open

contribute_api.description: POST endpoint to register the contribution after payment

receipt_header.description: Response header containing the signed receipt token after payment

instructions.description: Human-readable instructions for making a contribution

ApiSchema.description: Describes the routes, parameters, and request bodies an endpoint accepts. Attached to endpoints during creation and returned in 402 challenges, marketplace listings, and public endpoint metadata.

baseUrl.description: Optional base URL override for the API

ApiSchemaRoute.description: A single API route with its method, path, parameters, and optional request body

path.description: Route path with optional path parameters in curly braces

summary.description: Human-readable name shown to consumers on the payment page

example.description: Placeholder text shown in the input field
Default body content shown as placeholder

endpoint_creation_fee.description: Fee charged when agents deploy endpoints through this partner

StkStakeRole.description: Stake type. compute = compute node operator, validator = marketplace validator, yield = yield enjoyer.

transaction.description: Base64-encoded UNSIGNED legacy transaction. Sign with your keypair.

next.example: Sign each transaction with your wallet keypair, then POST /api/agent/submit { transaction }.

unlockAt.description: Unix ms cooldown end, or null.

claimableUnlock.description: True when unstaking cooldown has elapsed and tokens can be withdrawn.

StkManifest.description: Self-describing API manifest (name, program, roles, flow, endpoints).

enterpriseApiKey.name: X-Enterprise-Key
enterpriseApiKey.description: Enterprise partner API key with `sgl_ent_` prefix. Create keys in the Enterprise Dashboard under API Keys.

StkPrepare.description: Unsigned transaction(s) to sign and submit.
